slides.title: The *longevity vitamin* hiding in mushrooms
slides.sub: Ergothioneine is an amino acid your body cannot make, yet it gathers exactly where you need protection most.

slides.title: A nutrient you cannot make
slides.body: Ergothioneine is an amino acid we get only from food. Mushrooms are by far the *richest source* on your plate.

slides.title: It travels to stressed tissue
slides.body: Your cells use a dedicated transporter to pull ergothioneine into tissues under stress, where it *concentrates and waits*.

slides.title: A shield for mitochondria
slides.body: Once inside, it can support your mitochondria against oxidative stress. Researchers call it a *longevity vitamin* for this reason.

slides.title: Low levels track decline
slides.body: Lower blood ergothioneine is linked to faster cognitive decline as we age. Higher intake *may help* protect the brain.

slides.title: Eat more mushrooms
slides.body: A handful of cooked mushrooms a few times a week is an easy, *defensible habit* with real upside.

slides.title: Turn small habits into *real years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the food, sleep, movement and steps behind your longevity, then scores it all in one daily number.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
There is a nutrient your body cannot make, and mushrooms are the richest source of it. 🍄🌿

Ergothioneine gathers in stressed tissue, can support your mitochondria, and low levels are linked to faster cognitive decline. A few servings a week is an easy win. Save this. 🔖
